name: Dominik Nitsch
headline: Founder @ Generalyst | building talent infrastructure for early-stage startups | 1,306 generalists read my newsletter to future-proof their career.
text:
4/ There are wayyy more generalists out there than I thought

“Everything I read that you publish sounds like you wrote my internal monologue out loud.” — a subscriber

Not everybody wants to specialize. I knew that. 

But there are 10X more people who don’t to be boxed in, not just do one thing. Ev
llm_reasoning: Celebrating his newsletter's one-year milestone and community growth — no forcing moment, just retrospective pride and content marketing.

name: Anna Ferdinand
headline: Ich bringe dein Business in die Medien I Kommunikationsberatung & Training für Start-ups und Entrepreneure I Public Relations & Content mit Impact
text: Dominik Nitsch I feel you — the doubts, the waiting for those first real wins, the constant experimenting until things start clicking (and the content finally feels right). Congrats on one year of building Generalyst — that’s a huge milestone! 👏
llm_reasoning: Cheerleading on someone else's post with generic congrats — no own situation described, no forcing moment.

name: Julie Lepique
headline: Founder & CEO femtasy – The #1 audio pleasure platform with ​>2MN users | Building a next-gen media company | Capital Top 40 Under 40 | Forbes 30 Under 30 | OMR50 I Financial Times ‘Fastest Growing Companies’ I Speaker
text:
„Do something meaningful, then write about it.“ — 100% agree. For me, what I’m building / creating always comes first. Sharing about it on LinkedIn is a valuable / strategic byproduct.. but it will always remain a byproduct.

Happy first year!! :)
llm_reasoning: Senior founder with real scale, but comment is general content philosophy agreement — no forcing moment, no vulnerability, purely retrospective.
